name: Laurence F. Doud III
role_title: CEO, Rochester Drug Co-Operative (RDC)
charges: Conspiracy to unlawfully distribute oxycodone and fentanyl; conspiracy to defraud the DEA (first pharmaceutical wholesale CEO ever charged with drug trafficking conspiracy under the Controlled Substances Act)
outcome: 27 months in federal prison
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Doud sentencing
source2_description: NY Times coverage of pharmaceutical CEO conviction

name: Joel Smithers, M.D.
role_title: Physician (pain management), Martinsville, Virginia
charges: 466+ counts of illegally distributing Schedule II controlled substances, maintaining drug-distribution premises, causing patient death by opioid overdose (prescribed more than half a million opioid doses)
outcome: 40 years in federal prison
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Smithers retrial sentencing

name: Michael Babich
role_title: Former CEO, Insys Therapeutics
charges: Racketeering conspiracy (RICO), wire fraud conspiracy, mail fraud conspiracy — bribing physicians to prescribe fentanyl spray Subsys (cooperated against founder John Kapoor)
outcome: 30 months in federal prison
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Babich sentencing

name: Alec Burlakoff
role_title: Former VP of Sales, Insys Therapeutics
charges: Racketeering conspiracy (RICO), wire fraud — bribery of doctors to prescribe Subsys fentanyl spray (key cooperating witness against John Kapoor)
outcome: 26 months in federal prison
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Burlakoff sentencing

name: Charles Boyd
role_title: CEO, Safe Chain Solutions (pharmaceutical wholesale)
charges: Conspiracy to introduce misbranded drugs into interstate commerce, conspiracy to traffic medical products with false documentation, conspiracy to commit wire fraud, wire fraud — $92M black-market HIV drug diversion scheme
outcome: 20 years in federal prison; $21.85 million forfeiture
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Boyd sentencing

name: Patrick Boyd
role_title: Managing Partner (Sales), Safe Chain Solutions
charges: Conspiracy to introduce misbranded drugs into interstate commerce, conspiracy to traffic medical products with false documentation, conspiracy to commit wire fraud, wire fraud — $92M black-market HIV drug diversion scheme
outcome: 18 years in federal prison; $21.85 million forfeiture
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Patrick Boyd sentencing

name: Adam Brosius
role_title: Employee/co-conspirator, Safe Chain Solutions
charges: Conspiracy to commit wire fraud — black-market HIV drug diversion scheme endangering patients
outcome: 97 months in federal prison
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Brosius sentencing

name: Gary Cox
role_title: CEO, Power Mobility Doctor Rx LLC (DMERx) — healthcare software/telemedicine
charges: Conspiracy to commit healthcare fraud and wire fraud, 3 counts healthcare fraud, conspiracy to pay/receive healthcare kickbacks, conspiracy to defraud the United States — $1 billion in fraudulent Medicare claims (one of largest Medicare fraud cases ever tried)
outcome: 15 years in federal prison; $452 million restitution
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Cox sentencing

name: Sami Anwar
role_title: Owner/operator, clinical research companies, Richland, Washington
charges: 47 counts: wire fraud, mail fraud, conspiracy, fraudulently obtaining controlled substances, furnishing false material information to DEA — falsified human clinical trial data for cholesterol, diabetes, heart disease treatments
outcome: 28 years in federal prison; $1.9 million restitution; $5.6 million forfeiture
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Anwar sentencing

name: Dr. Yvelice Villaman Bencosme
role_title: Physician / clinical investigator, Unlimited Medical Research, Miami FL
charges: Conspiracy to commit wire fraud — falsified clinical trial data for asthma medication
outcome: 63 months in federal prison
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Bencosme sentencing

name: Miguel Angel Montalvo Villa
role_title: Co-owner, President & CEO, AMB Research Center Inc., Miami FL
charges: Conspiracy to commit wire fraud, wire fraud, making a false statement to an FDA investigator — falsified and fabricated clinical trial data; submitted fraudulent invoices to clinical trial sponsor
outcome: 71 months in federal prison
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Montalvo Villa sentencing

name: Dr. Dzung Ahn Pham
role_title: Physician / clinic owner, Irvine Village Urgent Care, Orange County CA
charges: Conspiracy to distribute controlled substances (oxycodone, hydrocodone, amphetamine salts) without legitimate medical purpose — issued ~150,000 controlled substance pills to known addicts over 5 years
outcome: 151 months / 12.5 years in federal prison
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Pham sentencing

name: Dr. Donald Siao
role_title: Family medicine physician, San Jose CA
charges: 12 counts distributing oxycodone and hydrocodone outside the usual course of professional practice and without a legitimate medical purpose — wrote 8,201 controlled substance prescriptions in a single year
outcome: Up to 240 years maximum exposure; sentencing Nov. 2023
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Siao conviction

name: Dr. Michael Taba
role_title: Orthopedic surgeon, McKinney TX
charges: Conspiracy to commit health care fraud, 3 counts health care fraud — $145 million compounding pharmacy kickback scheme; accepted bribes to prescribe unnecessary compound creams to injured federal workers billed at up to $16,000 per prescription
outcome: 102 months / 8.5 years in federal prison; $13 million restitution
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Taba sentencing

name: Dr. Adrian Dexter Talbot
role_title: Physician / clinic owner, Medex Clinical Consultants, Slidell LA
charges: Conspiracy to unlawfully distribute controlled substances, 4 counts unlawful distribution, maintaining drug-involved premises, conspiracy to commit health care fraud — 1.8 million opioid doses; pre-signed prescriptions for patients he never examined
outcome: 87 months / 7.25 years in federal prison
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Talbot sentencing

name: John Cruise
role_title: Co-owner, Assurance Consolidated Pharmacy (ACP) and IFWAA, Houston TX
charges: Conspiracy to defraud the United States — $126 million compounding pharmacy fraud scheme targeting DOL workers' compensation and TRICARE; medications chosen for high reimbursement, not medical need
outcome: 20 years in federal prison
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Cruise sentencing

name: LaShonia Johnson
role_title: Co-owner, Assurance Consolidated Pharmacy (ACP), Houston TX
charges: Conspiracy to defraud the United States — $126 million compounding pharmacy fraud scheme targeting workers' compensation and military healthcare
outcome: 15 years in federal prison
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Johnson sentencing

name: Kenny Ozoude
role_title: Owner, Compounding Solutions LLC, Houston TX
charges: Conspiracy to defraud the United States — $126 million compounding pharmacy fraud; pharmacy charged up to $16,000 per compound cream that cost $15 to produce
outcome: 10 years in federal prison
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Ozoude sentencing

name: James Don Jackson, M.D.
role_title: Physician (Texas), Compounding Solutions LLC fraud scheme, Tyler TX
charges: Receiving health care kickbacks — accepted bribes to prescribe unnecessary compound creams to injured federal workers and military members; part of $126 million fraud
outcome: 5 years in federal prison
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Jackson sentencing

name: Petros Fichidzhyan
role_title: Operator, sham hospice companies, Granada Hills CA
charges: Healthcare fraud, aggravated identity theft, concealment money laundering — $16 million Medicare hospice fraud; billed for patients who were not terminally ill
outcome: 12 years in federal prison; $17.1 million restitution
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Fichidzhyan sentencing

name: Quintan Cockerell
role_title: Pharmaceutical marketer, Palos Verdes Estates CA (operating in Texas)
charges: Conspiracy to defraud the United States, receiving illegal kickbacks for compounded medication prescription referrals, money laundering — created and marketed expensive compounded medications not medically indicated
outcome: 29 months in federal prison; $59 million restitution
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Cockerell sentencing

name: Jennings Ryan Staley, M.D.
role_title: Physician, San Diego CA
charges: Importation contrary to law — attempted to smuggle hydroxychloroquine to sell as a COVID-19 'miracle cure'; worked with Chinese supplier to mislabel shipments as 'yam extract'
outcome: 30 days custody; 1 year home confinement; $10,000 fine
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Staley sentencing

name: Gordon Hunter Pedersen
role_title: Self-described 'doctor,' CEO, My Doctor Suggests LLC, Cedar Hills UT (not a licensed physician)
charges: Mail fraud, introduction of misbranded drugs into interstate commerce with intent to defraud — sold silver products as a COVID-19 'cure' for multiple conditions
outcome: 36 months in federal prison
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Pedersen sentencing

name: Jeffrey Paul Madison
role_title: CEO, hospital operator, Burnet TX
charges: Conspiracy to violate the Anti-Kickback Statute — made illegal payments to physicians for laboratory referrals; part of five-defendant hospital executive conviction
outcome: 36 months in federal prison; $5.3 million False Claims Act settlement
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Madison sentencing

name: Dr. Robert Florea
role_title: Physician / clinic owner, Buckeye Health and Research, Columbus OH
charges: Medicaid fraud — claimed reimbursement for 5,100+ pieces of equipment but purchased only 460 (billed $1.5 million in false claims)
outcome: 24 months; $1.5 million restitution; permanent medical license surrender
source1_description: Ohio Attorney General press release

name: Kireyus Wilson
role_title: Owner, telemedicine companies
charges: Conspiracy to commit wire fraud and health care fraud — $56 million Medicare fraud scheme for unnecessary durable medical equipment; submitted over $56 million in false Medicare claims in two years
outcome: 7 years in federal prison; $27.9 million restitution
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Wilson sentencing

name: Mohamed Hassan
role_title: Licensed pharmacist, Brooklyn NY
charges: Conspiracy to dispense and distribute oxycodone, distribution and possession with intent to distribute oxycodone — filled fake prescriptions written by a doctor's receptionist for street drug dealers
outcome: 18 years in federal prison
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Hassan sentencing

name: Yousef Ennab
role_title: Licensed pharmacist, Brooklyn NY
charges: Conspiracy to dispense and distribute oxycodone, distribution and possession with intent to distribute oxycodone — filled fake oxycodone prescriptions for street drug dealers
outcome: 30 months in federal prison
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Ennab sentencing

name: Carolyn Bryant-Taylor
role_title: Co-owner, 1st Adult N Pediatric Healthcare Services, Clinton MD
charges: Conspiracy to commit health care fraud — $10 million Virginia Medicaid fraud scheme over six years; submitted false claims for home health services never provided
outcome: 10 years in federal prison; $10 million restitution jointly
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Bryant-Taylor sentencing

name: Kafomdi Josephine Okocha
role_title: Co-owner, 1st Adult N Pediatric Healthcare Services, Upper Marlboro MD
charges: Conspiracy to commit health care fraud — $10 million Virginia Medicaid fraud
outcome: 8 years in federal prison; $10 million restitution jointly
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Okocha sentencing

name: Samuel Okocha
role_title: Co-owner, 1st Adult N Pediatric Healthcare Services, Upper Marlboro MD
charges: Conspiracy to commit health care fraud — $10 million Virginia Medicaid fraud
outcome: 6 years in federal prison; $10 million restitution jointly
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Samuel Okocha sentencing

name: Dr. Victor Contreras
role_title: Physician, Santa Paula CA (worked for Pasadena hospices)
charges: Health care fraud — billed Medicare $3.2 million for medically unnecessary hospice services; falsely certified patients as terminally ill
outcome: 24 months in federal prison; $3.3 million restitution
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Contreras sentencing

name: Oscar B. Abrons III
role_title: Healthcare clinic operator, God's Property clinic (unlicensed), Southern California
charges: Prescription drug diversion — paid Medi-Cal beneficiaries cash for unnecessary prescriptions for HIV medications, antipsychotics, and controlled substances; diverted medications to illicit market
outcome: 4 years; $20 million+ restitution to Medi-Cal
source1_description: California Attorney General press release

name: Larry Nassar
role_title: Team USA Gymnastics National Team Physician; Michigan State University Osteopathic Physician
charges: Federal: possession of child pornography (37,000+ images). State: 7 counts of criminal sexual conduct in the first degree (Ingham County); 3 counts of criminal sexual conduct (Eaton County) — sexually abused hundreds of young female athletes under guise of medical treatment
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, W.D. Michigan (federal); Ingham County Circuit Court, Michigan (state); Eaton County Circuit Court, Michigan (state)
outcome: Federal: 60 years in prison (December 7, 2017). Ingham County: 40–175 years (January 24, 2018). Eaton County: 40–125 years concurrent. Effectively: life in prison.
conviction_date: Federal: November 22, 2017 (guilty plea); Ingham: January 24, 2018 (sentencing); Eaton: February 5, 2018 (sentencing)
source1_description: DOJ W.D. Michigan — Larry Nassar sentenced to 60 years for child pornography
source2_description: ESPN — Larry Nassar sentenced up to 175 years in sex abuse case
notes: Over 500 victims have come forward. USA Gymnastics and Michigan State University have paid hundreds of millions in settlements. The scandal led to criminal charges against multiple officials who knew and failed to act.

name: Conrad Murray, M.D.
role_title: Personal Physician to Michael Jackson
charges: Involuntary manslaughter — administered fatal doses of propofol (a surgical anesthetic) as a sleep aid, causing the death of Michael Jackson on June 25, 2009
jurisdiction: Los Angeles County Superior Court, California (state)
outcome: Convicted; sentenced to maximum 4 years in county jail. Released after 2 years due to jail overcrowding, October 28, 2013.
source1_description: CNN — Conrad Murray convicted of Michael Jackson's death
source2_description: BBC News — Conrad Murray released from prison

name: William Husel, M.D.
role_title: Physician, Mount Carmel Health System, Columbus Ohio
charges: 25 counts of murder in the first degree — administered what prosecutors called fatal overdoses of fentanyl (up to 2,000 mcg) to critically ill patients, allegedly to hasten their deaths
jurisdiction: Franklin County Common Pleas Court, Ohio (state)
outcome: Acquitted of all 14 remaining murder counts (after 11 counts were dismissed pre-trial); jury found not guilty February 2022. Surrendered medical license.
conviction_date: February 15, 2022 (jury verdict — ACQUITTED on all remaining counts)
source1_description: AP News — Ohio doctor acquitted of murder charges in patient deaths
source2_description: Columbus Dispatch — Husel found not guilty in all 14 patient death cases
notes: Husel surrendered his Ohio medical license. A civil lawsuit by Mount Carmel Health System was settled. Despite acquittal, he was fired and his license surrendered. The case raised major questions about end-of-life care standards.
